retrieval_and_qa.answer:
The document says Endeavor is presented as a nonprofit founded by Linda Rottenberg and Peter Kellner, framed around entrepreneurship in emerging markets, especially Latin America, while operating as a bridge for ambitious founders across places including Buenos Aires, New York, Washington, Silicon Valley, and Davos. [CITATION|chunk_id=file:///Users/ash/Documents/repos/Zoomlytics/power-atlas/demo/fixtures/datasets/demo_dataset_v1/unstructured/chain_of_custody.pdf:0|run_id=unstructured_ingest-20260415T221145903438Z-0153cd6c|source_uri=file:///Users/ash/Documents/repos/Zoomlytics/power-atlas/demo/fixtures/datasets/demo_dataset_v1/unstructured/chain_of_custody.pdf|chunk_index=0|page=1|start_char=0|end_char=992]

It also says the story creates the impression that Endeavor was “less a simple nonprofit than a relay point linking financiers, tech executives, political operators, and ambitious founders across the Americas.” [CITATION|chunk_id=file:///Users/ash/Documents/repos/Zoomlytics/power-atlas/demo/fixtures/datasets/demo_dataset_v1/unstructured/chain_of_custody.pdf:2|run_id=unstructured_ingest-20260415T221145903438Z-0153cd6c|source_uri=file:///Users/ash/Documents/repos/Zoomlytics/power-atlas/demo/fixtures/datasets/demo_dataset_v1/unstructured/chain_of_custody.pdf|chunk_index=2|page=1|start_char=1600|end_char=2597]

Regarding MercadoLibre, the document says Endeavor Argentina drew in Marcos Galperin of MercadoLibre, and that MercadoLibre became the most visible success story in this network. [CITATION|chunk_id=file:///Users/ash/Documents/repos/Zoomlytics/power-atlas/demo/fixtures/datasets/demo_dataset_v1/unstructured/chain_of_custody.pdf:2|run_id=unstructured_ingest-20260415T221145903438Z-0153cd6c|source_uri=file:///Users/ash/Documents/repos/Zoomlytics/power-atlas/demo/fixtures/datasets/demo_dataset_v1/unstructured/chain_of_custody.pdf|chunk_index=2|page=1|start_char=1600|end_char=2597]

The document describes MercadoLibre as “an e-commerce and payments giant often compared to Amazon and eBay,” with MercadoPago extending its reach into digital payments and crypto-adjacent finance. [CITATION|chunk_id=file:///Users/ash/Documents/repos/Zoomlytics/power-atlas/demo/fixtures/datasets/demo_dataset_v1/unstructured/chain_of_custody.pdf:3|run_id=unstructured_ingest-20260415T221145903438Z-0153cd6c|source_uri=file:///Users/ash/Documents/repos/Zoomlytics/power-atlas/demo/fixtures/datasets/demo_dataset_v1/unstructured/chain_of_custody.pdf|chunk_index=3|page=1|start_char=2389|end_char=3385]

The document further says MercadoLibre was tied in the article to Paxos, PYUSD, Ripio, Mercado Bitcoin, and a wider payments-and-stablecoin corridor running through Argentina and Brazil. [CITATION|chunk_id=file:///Users/ash/Documents/repos/Zoomlytics/power-atlas/demo/fixtures/datasets/demo_dataset_v1/unstructured/chain_of_custody.pdf:3|run_id=unstructured_ingest-20260415T221145903438Z-0153cd6c|source_uri=file:///Users/ash/Documents/repos/Zoomlytics/power-atlas/demo/fixtures/datasets/demo_dataset_v1/unstructured/chain_of_custody.pdf|chunk_index=3|page=1|start_char=2389|end_char=3385]

In the condensed narrative, Endeavor is described as operating “as a multiplier system as much as an accelerator,” and one example path says a founder might meet mentors through Endeavor Argentina and later sit on boards linked to MercadoLibre. [CITATION|chunk_id=file:///Users/ash/Documents/repos/Zoomlytics/power-atlas/demo/fixtures/datasets/demo_dataset_v1/unstructured/chain_of_custody.pdf:8|run_id=unstructured_ingest-20260415T221145903438Z-0153cd6c|source_uri=file:///Users/ash/Documents/repos/Zoomlytics/power-atlas/demo/fixtures/datasets/demo_dataset_v1/unstructured/chain_of_custody.pdf|chunk_index=8|page=2|start_char=6400|end_char=7396]

retrieval_and_qa.retrieval_query_contract:
WITH node AS c, score
WHERE c.run_id = $run_id
  AND ($source_uri IS NULL OR c.source_uri = $source_uri)
RETURN c.text AS chunk_text,
       c.chunk_id AS chunk_id,
       c.run_id AS run_id,
       c.source_uri AS source_uri,
       c.chunk_index AS chunk_index,
       coalesce(c.page_number, c.page) AS page,
       c.start_char AS start_char,
       c.end_char AS end_char,
       score AS similarityScore

retrieval_results.content:
broader PayPal mafia, became another recurring figure. Around them were institutions
such as the Council on Foreign Relations, the World Economic Forum, the Aspen Institute, Bain Capital,
Goldman Sachs, J.P . Morgan, eBay, PayPal, and In-Q-tel. The story moved easily between philanthropy,
venture capital, public policy, and digital infrastructure, creating the impression that Endeavor was less a
simple nonprofit than a relay point linking financiers, tech executives, political operators, and ambitious
founders across the Americas.
Argentina became one of the clearest stages for this network. Endeavor Argentina drew in Marcos Galperin
of MercadoLibre, Guibert Englebienne and Martin Migoya of Globant, Maria Julia Bearzi, Eduardo Elzstain,
and later figures orbiting Javier Milei’s business-friendly circles. Galperin’s MercadoLibre became the most
visible  success  story:  an  e-commerce  and  payments  giant  often  compared  to  Amazon  and  eBay,  with
MercadoPago extending its reach 
[CITATION|chunk_id=file:///Users/ash/Documents/repos/Zoomlytics/power-atlas/demo/fixtures/datasets/demo_dataset_v1/unstructured/chain_of_custody.pdf:2|run_id=unstructured_ingest-20260415T221145903438Z-0153cd6c|source_uri=file:///Users/ash/Documents/repos/Zoomlytics/power-atlas/demo/fixtures/datasets/demo_dataset_v1/unstructured/chain_of_custody.pdf|chunk_index=2|page=1|start_char=1600|end_char=2597]

ork, and the World Economic Forum sat near founders
building payments companies, crypto exchanges, AI consultancies, and e-commerce platforms. For a graph
database demo, that mixture is useful because a user can jump from a person to an organization, from an
organization to an investor , from an investor to a country, and from a country to a sector such as crypto, AI,
logistics, or digital identity.
Read as a condensed narrative, the story suggests that Endeavor operated as a multiplier system as much
as an accelerator . A founder might begin in Buenos Aires, meet mentors through Endeavor Argentina, raise
capital from investors in New York or San Francisco, sell to Santander , partner with eBay or PayPal, appear
at the Llao Llao Forum with Eduardo Elzstain and Javier Milei, and later sit on boards linked to Microsoft,
MercadoLibre, Globant, Xapo, Ripio, Kaszek, or Tether-connected ventures. Linda Rottenberg, Peter Kellner ,
Edgar Bronfman Jr ., Pierre Omidyar , Reid Hoffman, Marcos 
[CITATION|chunk_id=file:///Users/ash/Documents/repos/Zoomlytics/power-atlas/demo/fixtures/datasets/demo_dataset_v1/unstructured/chain_of_custody.pdf:8|run_id=unstructured_ingest-20260415T221145903438Z-0153cd6c|source_uri=file:///Users/ash/Documents/repos/Zoomlytics/power-atlas/demo/fixtures/datasets/demo_dataset_v1/unstructured/chain_of_custody.pdf|chunk_index=8|page=2|start_char=6400|end_char=7396]

business-friendly circles. Galperin’s MercadoLibre became the most
visible  success  story:  an  e-commerce  and  payments  giant  often  compared  to  Amazon  and  eBay,  with
MercadoPago extending its reach into digital payments and crypto-adjacent finance. The article also tied
MercadoLibre  to  Paxos,  PYUSD,  Ripio,  Mercado  Bitcoin,  and  a  wider  payments-and-stablecoin  corridor
running  through  Argentina  and  Brazil.  Nearby  sat  Banco  Hipotecario  and  IRSA,  both  associated  with
Elzstain, along with the Llao Llao Forum, where business elites, startup founders, and political figures
gathered in Patagonia to discuss cryptocurrency, artificial intelligence, and generational change.
Another  branch  of  the  story  ran  through  Wences  Casares,  Patagon,  and  later  Xapo.  Endeavor  helped
arrange roadshows that introduced Casares to investors in New York, Boston, and San Francisco, and
Patagon was later sold to Santander . In this compressed version, Casares looks 
[CITATION|chunk_id=file:///Users/ash/Documents/repos/Zoomlytics/power-atlas/demo/fixtures/datasets/demo_dataset_v1/unstructured/chain_of_custody.pdf:3|run_id=unstructured_ingest-20260415T221145903438Z-0153cd6c|source_uri=file:///Users/ash/Documents/repos/Zoomlytics/power-atlas/demo/fixtures/datasets/demo_dataset_v1/unstructured/chain_of_custody.pdf|chunk_index=3|page=1|start_char=2389|end_char=3385]

The Endeavor Network: A Simplified Narrative for
Graph Demo Ingestion
Condensed unstructured sample adapted from the attached source article
Demo note: This version is intentionally simplified and lightly fictionalized for entity extraction, relationship
extraction, and graph retrieval testing. It preserves many of the people, companies, places, and institutional
links mentioned in the source while keeping a narrative, article-like style.
In  the  late  1990s,  a  network  began  to  take  shape  around  Endeavor ,  the  nonprofit  founded  by  Linda
Rottenberg and Peter Kellner . The project was framed as entrepreneurship for emerging markets, especially
Latin America, but the cast around it quickly stretched from Buenos Aires and Santiago to New York,
Washington, Silicon Valley, and Davos. Rottenberg, who had worked in Argentina and moved through circles
connected  to  Ashoka  and  Bill  Drayton,  presented  Endeavor  as  a  bridge  for  founders  who  were  too
ambitious for 
[CITATION|chunk_id=file:///Users/ash/Documents/repos/Zoomlytics/power-atlas/demo/fixtures/datasets/demo_dataset_v1/unstructured/chain_of_custody.pdf:0|run_id=unstructured_ingest-20260415T221145903438Z-0153cd6c|source_uri=file:///Users/ash/Documents/repos/Zoomlytics/power-atlas/demo/fixtures/datasets/demo_dataset_v1/unstructured/chain_of_custody.pdf|chunk_index=0|page=1|start_char=0|end_char=992]

pillar to the network. Founded in Buenos Aires, it grew into a software, AI, and
digital transformation company serving clients such as Google, Disney, Electronic Arts, Nestle, American
Express, and Santander Bank. Martin Migoya spoke about preparing institutions for a digital and cognitive
future, while the company’s leadership appeared beside Endeavor , the Council of the Americas, Microsoft,
and major banks. In the source material, Globant did not stand alone; it shared a stage with MercadoLibre
and  with  public-sector  modernization,  turning  the  company  into  a  useful  node  for  demos  about  how
corporate service firms sit between governments, platforms, cloud vendors, and the AI boom. A graph built
from this material could connect Globant not just to Argentina but to California, New York, London, and
Madrid through clients, investors, directors, and policy networks.
The  cast  widened  further  with  Kiva,  Better  Than  Cash  Alliance,  the  United  Nations,  the  
[CITATION|chunk_id=file:///Users/ash/Documents/repos/Zoomlytics/power-atlas/demo/fixtures/datasets/demo_dataset_v1/unstructured/chain_of_custody.pdf:5|run_id=unstructured_ingest-20260415T221145903438Z-0153cd6c|source_uri=file:///Users/ash/Documents/repos/Zoomlytics/power-atlas/demo/fixtures/datasets/demo_dataset_v1/unstructured/chain_of_custody.pdf|chunk_index=5|page=2|start_char=3999|end_char=4991]
